task_completion.description: Create the conditions CSV file with 'offer' and 'keep' columns and 5 rows summing to 10.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application showing a file named 'ug_conditions.csv' with headers 'offer' and 'keep', and 5 rows of numeric data where each row's values sum to exactly 10.

task_completion.description: Save the PsychoPy experiment as ultimatum_game.psyexp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder window title bar displays 'ultimatum_game.psyexp' indicating the file has been saved with the correct name.

task_completion.description: Create instruction, trial, and feedback routines in the Flow.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel at the bottom of the PsychoPy window shows three distinct routines, typically named 'instruction', 'trial', and 'feedback' (or similar), in that order.

task_completion.description: Wrap the trial routine in a loop linked to the conditions file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel shows a loop (e.g., 'trials') surrounding the trial routine. A Loop Properties dialog may be visible showing 'ug_conditions.csv' loaded in the 'Conditions' field with 5 conditions recognized.

task_completion.description: Add text components for offer/keep and a keyboard component for 'y'/'n' responses to the trial routine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The trial routine tab shows Text components (to display the offer/keep variables) and a Keyboard component (configured to accept 'y' and 'n' keys).

task_completion.description: Add a code component to calculate earnings and a text component to display feedback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Code component is visible in the trial or feedback routine (containing logic to check if the response was 'y' or 'n' and set earnings), and a Text component is present in the feedback routine to display the resulting message.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to create the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding routines, components, editing properties) rather than writing XML/psyexp code directly in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI text editor or spreadsheet tool to create the CSV.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the CSV being edited in a text editor (like gedit, nano) or spreadsheet app, rather than being generated solely via terminal echo/cat commands.

integrity.description: The agent used variables from the conditions file rather than hardcoding text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Text components in PsychoPy show variable syntax (e.g., $offer, $keep) with 'set every repeat' selected, rather than hardcoded numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment file must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ultimatum_game.psyexp'. The conditions file must be saved at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ug_conditions.csv'. The CSV must contain exactly the columns 'offer' and 'keep', and have at least 5 valid rows where the offer and keep values sum to 10.
